Hook strength.rationale: Names a specific date (May 20th), a concrete event (regular session close), and immediately identifies a missing framing (the gap the press missed). Hits the rubric's 10-descriptor: concrete, specific, names a live tension, hints at a missing option. No banned phrases.

Local relevance.rationale: Every story is anchored to named Alaskan entities: Sen. Löki Tobin, Railbelt grid, Chugach Electric, Golden Valley Electric, Cook Inlet, JBER, Eielson, Clear SFS, Fairbanks spur line. No imported national story present. Satisfies the 10-descriptor fully.

Factual density.rationale: Specifics appear every 1-2 sentences throughout: dates (May 16th-21st, 29th), vote tallies (14-5, 20-0), dollar figures ($40M fund), tax rates (6/15/40 cents), named entities in every paragraph.

Source quality.rationale: Cook Inlet/compute claim explicitly attributed to Chugach Electric, Golden Valley Electric, and ADN. SB 250 and HB 239 vote tallies are primary legislative record. Governor's-office concerns and 'overwhelming' House margin characterization lack explicit inline source attribution — places this at the 7-descriptor.

Voice match.rationale: Mirrors post_001.md structurally and tonally: counter-framing move ('That framing is incomplete'), structural problem named explicitly, position taken without hedging, plainspoken sentences, curly quotes, no hashtags, no emojis, no banned phrases.

Readability (mobile).rationale: Approximately 355 words within the 280-420 target. Paragraphs are short (3-5 sentences). The Air Force deadline is a standalone sentence. No wall-of-text block. Easy to scan on a phone.

Engagement question.rationale: Closing question is real, debatable, and tied directly to the specific structural tension: HB 239 passing vs. SB 250 dying. Not generic, no follow-us CTA.
